groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Popup goal, subject, audience, facts, desired action, page context, destination, trigger, tone, and output language.

groups.title: Offer & Conversion
groups.description: Incentive, real deadline or availability, and neutral secondary-action controls.

groups.title: Brand & Experience
groups.description: Brand identification, voice guidance, popup format, and copy-length preference.

groups.title: Privacy & Output
groups.description: Collected data, privacy link, restrictions, variant count, and remaining context.

variables.label: Popup goal
variables.hint: Choose the single most important result this popup should produce.

help.what: Provide the popup goal for this website popup.
help.why: The goal determines the popup structure, persuasive angle, CTA, and suitable trigger.
help.avoid: Avoid mixing several goals, such as list growth, feedback, and product traffic, inside one popup.

options: Drive to a page or product

variables.label: Offer or message
variables.placeholder: Example: 15% welcome discount for first-time customers
variables.hint: Describe what the popup is promoting, announcing, offering, or requesting.

help.what: Provide the offer or message for this website popup.
help.why: The model cannot safely infer the actual offer, announcement, resource, or message.
help.example: Good example: 15% welcome discount for first-time customers
help.avoid: Avoid vague topics, invented offers, fake discounts, or messages that are not actually approved.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Example: First-time visitors comparing affordable project-management tools
variables.hint: Describe the visitors who should see the popup and what matters to them.

help.what: Provide the target audience for this website popup.
help.why: Audience context materially changes the value proposition, vocabulary, and level of persuasion.
help.example: Good example: First-time visitors comparing affordable project-management tools
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported claims, multiple competing messages, or changing the popup into a different campaign.

variables.label: Essential facts
variables.placeholder: Include benefits, conditions, dates, eligibility, prices, or other confirmed details
variables.hint: Provide only facts the generated popup may safely state as true.

help.what: Provide the essential facts for this website popup.
help.why: It supplies the factual basis and prevents the model from inventing claims or offer terms.
help.example: Good example: The discount applies to the first monthly subscription, excludes annual plans, and expires August 31, 2026.
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported claims, fake urgency, invented prices, unverified deadlines, or missing terms.

variables.label: Desired visitor action
variables.placeholder: Example: Enter an email address and receive the guide
variables.hint: State the one primary action visitors should take after reading the popup.

help.what: Provide the desired visitor action for this website popup.
help.why: The primary CTA must reflect the user's actual conversion objective.
help.example: Good example: Enter an email address and receive the guide
help.avoid: Avoid several competing actions or CTA wording that hides what happens next.

variables.label: Page or journey context
variables.placeholder: Example: Pricing page after the visitor scrolls through plan comparisons
variables.hint: Explain where the popup appears or what the visitor was doing beforehand.

help.what: Optional context for the page or journey context field.
help.why: Page context helps the popup feel relevant rather than generic or disruptive.
help.example: Good example: Pricing page after the visitor scrolls through plan comparisons
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Destination URL
variables.hint: Add the page opened by the primary CTA when the action leads elsewhere.

help.what: Optional context for the destination url field.
help.why: It keeps the CTA and implementation recommendation aligned with the real destination.
help.avoid: Avoid invented, shortened, rewritten, tracking-modified, or unsupported links.

variables.label: Popup trigger
variables.hint: Choose a trigger or let the model recommend the least disruptive option.

help.what: Optional context for the popup trigger field.
help.why: Trigger context changes the opening message and the appropriate level of urgency.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Tone
variables.hint: Choose the overall voice without controlling individual sentences.

help.what: Optional context for the tone field.
help.why: Tone materially affects the copy while remaining simple enough for non-specialist users.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Output language
variables.hint: Enter the language in which the complete popup package should be written.

help.what: Optional context for the output language field.
help.why: A free-text language field supports any language without an oversized select menu.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Incentive type
variables.hint: Choose the real incentive connected to the visitor action.

help.what: Optional context for the incentive type field.
help.why: It lets the prompt distinguish between an ordinary message and a value exchange.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Incentive details
variables.placeholder: Example: 15% off the first order, excluding gift cards
variables.hint: Provide the exact value, conditions, code, exclusions, or delivery method.

help.what: Optional context for the incentive details field.
help.why: Specific incentive terms must come from the user rather than being fabricated.
help.example: Good example: 15% off the first order, excluding gift cards
help.avoid: Avoid invented discounts, bonuses, eligibility, shipping terms, delivery promises, or offer conditions.

variables.label: Real deadline or availability
variables.placeholder: Example: Valid until August 31, 2026, while the event registration is open
variables.hint: Enter only a genuine deadline, stock condition, schedule, or availability limit.

help.what: Optional context for the real deadline or availability field.
help.why: It allows legitimate urgency while preventing invented countdowns or scarcity.
help.example: Good example: Valid until August 31, 2026, while registration is open
help.avoid: Avoid countdowns, last-chance language, stock claims, or scarcity that is not a real supplied fact.

variables.label: Secondary action
variables.hint: Control the optional alternative to the primary CTA.

help.what: Optional context for the secondary action field.
help.why: Dismissal wording affects user trust and must not become manipulative.
help.avoid: Avoid confirmshaming, guilt-based dismissals, hidden close controls, or manipulative alternatives.

variables.label: Custom secondary text
variables.hint: Enter a neutral alternative action without guilt-based wording.

help.what: Optional context for the custom secondary text field.
help.why: It supports a legitimate custom path without loading an unnecessary field for every user.
help.avoid: Avoid confirmshaming, guilt-based dismissals, hidden close controls, or manipulative alternatives.

variables.label: Brand name
variables.hint: Add the brand name only when recognition inside the popup is useful.

help.what: Optional context for the brand name field.
help.why: Brand identification may improve clarity but is unnecessary for every popup.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Brand voice guidance
variables.placeholder: Example: Direct, practical, lightly conversational, never overly enthusiastic
variables.hint: Describe established voice rules or provide a short example.

help.what: Optional context for the brand voice guidance field.
help.why: It allows established brands to preserve a specific voice beyond a general tone selection.
help.example: Good example: Direct, practical, lightly conversational, never overly enthusiastic
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Popup format
variables.hint: Choose the interface type or let the model recommend one.

help.what: Optional context for the popup format field.
help.why: The available space and interruption level directly affect copy length and structure.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Copy length
variables.hint: Control the amount of copy without requiring exact character knowledge.

help.what: Optional context for the copy length field.
help.why: Different popup formats and offers require different levels of explanation.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Data collected
variables.hint: List only the information visitors will actually be asked to submit.

help.what: Optional context for the data collected field.
help.why: The generated form and privacy microcopy must reflect the real requested data.
help.example: Good example: Email address, first name
help.avoid: Avoid requesting sensitive, unnecessary, or undisclosed data fields.

variables.label: Privacy policy URL
variables.hint: Provide the policy link used near a signup or data-collection form.

help.what: Optional context for the privacy policy url field.
help.why: It enables useful privacy-link guidance without inventing a policy or compliance claim.
help.avoid: Avoid fake policy links, unsupported privacy promises, or presenting legal compliance as confirmed.

variables.label: Restrictions and claims to avoid
variables.placeholder: List prohibited wording, legal restrictions, exclusions, or brand rules
variables.hint: Combine all project-specific restrictions in one place.

help.what: Optional context for the restrictions and claims to avoid field.
help.why: The user may know legal, contractual, product, or brand limitations the model cannot infer.
help.example: Good example: No fake scarcity, no guaranteed results, and no unsupported privacy promises.
help.avoid: Avoid ignoring known legal, brand, product, or contractual restrictions.

variables.label: Number of variants
variables.hint: Generate several genuinely different angles without creating an excessive package.

help.what: Optional context for the number of variants field.
help.why: It controls output volume and supports practical testing or comparison.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Additional context
variables.placeholder: Add any remaining requirement that materially affects the popup
variables.hint: Use this for important context not covered by another field.

help.what: Optional context for the additional context field.
help.why: It handles legitimate edge cases without creating several low-value miscellaneous fields.
help.example: Good example: Keep mobile copy short and avoid intrusive language.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported claims, hidden promotion, or sensitive private data.
